  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "The tension in each sling leg is 4,000 lb, which equals the vertical Working Load Limit (WLL) of 4,000 lb.",
    "Operating at 100% of the vertical WLL at a 30-degree horizontal angle leaves no safety margin, violating standard rigging safety practices."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Reject the current rigging plan.",
    "Select slings with a higher Working Load Limit (WLL) or increase the horizontal sling angle to reduce leg tension."
  ],
  "rationale": "For a symmetric two-leg sling arrangement, the tension in each leg is calculated as (Load / 2) / sin(horizontal angle). Here, (4,000 lb / 2) / sin(30 degrees) = 2,000 / 0.5 = 4,000 lb. Since the tension in each leg (4,000 lb) equals the vertical WLL of the sling (4,000 lb), and rigging at a 30-degree angle significantly reduces the rated capacity of the sling compared to its vertical rating, this configuration is unsafe and non-compliant.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.9",
    "OSHA 1926.251"
  ]
}
